action.hypothesis: The current best family may need cleaner support suppression rather than another additive or multiplicative residual path. An adaptive-threshold TopK encoder that keeps the proven linear logits as the anchor but learns an input-dependent nonnegative per-latent suppression term could reduce false activations at K=128 and materially improve FVU without abandoning the strong baseline initialization.
action.summary: The gated probe was a clear regression, so this round moves to a new architecture edit instead of more parameter-only tuning on weak recent families. The new `adaptive_threshold_topk` family starts exactly from plain TopK behavior and only learns to suppress linear logits, making it a focused quality-first prototype to test at the K=128/ef=8/Adam anchor under proxy.
action.self_review: This is a single coherent architectural bet grounded in the strongest observed prior: keeping the linear TopK path intact. The main risk is that suppression-only flexibility may still be too weak to beat hybrid_topk, but it is safer and more targeted than the recent families that replaced or loosely mixed the baseline encoder.
notes_to_memory: Round 14 adds a new adaptive_threshold_topk family that preserves the plain linear TopK encoder at initialization and learns only input-dependent nonnegative suppression of latent logits.
A quick local import/forward sanity check passed for adaptive_threshold_topk before handing control back to the runtime.
next_hypotheses: If adaptive_threshold_topk shows a strong proxy gain, promote it to full as the next mainline quality candidate.

If adaptive_threshold_topk is flat or weak, the next round should bias toward a more expressive new architecture or a hybrid-family training-dynamics experiment rather than revisiting gated or jumprelu local sweeps.
